meta.title: PicoMUTX — Get an AI Agent Working and Under Control
meta.description: PicoMUTX helps founders and operators build, recover, and control AI agents they can actually trust in real-world use.

hero.title: Get an AI agent working
hero.titleAccent: and trust it in real work.
hero.subtitle: Start from zero, recover a broken setup, or take control of a live agent. Pico routes you into build, fix, or control.
hero.meta: Start immediately. Pico diagnoses the blocker and routes you into build, fix, or control.

items.0: Start from zero, a messy build, or a live system
items.1: One diagnosis layer routes you into build, fix, or control
items.2: Visibility, approvals, and safeguards before trust is assumed

problem.title: Most agent projects do not fail
problem.body: Projects stall when the path gets messy: too many stack decisions, no recovery path, and no operational clarity once something breaks.

0.label: "I know what I want the agent to do. I still need the fastest sane path to get one working."
0.body: Starting from zero is not the real problem. The real problem is choosing the stack, sequence, and standards without turning the work into endless research.

1.label: "I already built part of this. Now I need to recover the setup instead of restarting from scratch."
1.body: When a build breaks halfway through, losing momentum costs more than time. You need a recovery lane, not another theory rabbit hole.

2.label: "Something is already live, but I do not trust the cost, logic, or failure modes yet."
2.body: At that point it is not enough for the agent to exist. It has to be legible, controllable, and safe enough to touch real work.

3.label: "I need a system I can explain, review, and control before I rely on it."
3.body: If you cannot see runs, thresholds, approvals, and behavior under pressure, you do not have an operational asset yet.

problem.close: Pico turns that uncertainty into one honest next move.

platform.title: Pico diagnoses the blocker, then routes you to build, fix, or control.
platform.body: Get something useful working and keep it trustworthy. Pico starts with diagnosis so the next move matches the real blocker.

0.title: Describe the goal or connect the agent
0.body: Start with the job, the blocker, or the live system you need to understand. Pico uses that context to decide what kind of help is actually relevant.

1.title: Get routed into build, fix, or control
1.body: New builds get a practical path to one useful agent. Broken setups get recovery steps. Live systems get visibility, thresholds, approvals, and operator guardrails.

2.title: Apply changes in one operational loop
2.body: Observe behavior, isolate what is off, implement the next change, and keep moving without losing the thread.

3.title: Escalate when the edge case is real
3.body: When the system cannot close the loop alone, human guidance steps in as proof and calibration, not as the entire product.

who.title: PicoMUTX is not for everyone.
who.notForYouTitle: This is not for you if…

forYou.0: You want to ship one useful agent without assembling the whole stack by guesswork
forYou.1: You already have a half-built or broken setup and need to recover momentum
forYou.2: You have something live or nearly live and need more control before you trust it
forYou.3: You want outcome-first help, not a course or theory rabbit hole
forYou.4: You want a system that reduces dependence on ad hoc expert intervention over time

notForYou.0: You want a generic no-code toy that removes judgment from the work
notForYou.1: You already need enterprise-grade governance infrastructure across a mature agent estate
notForYou.2: You want education, hype, or trend-chasing more than operational progress
notForYou.3: You expect AI to replace ownership, review, or accountability
notForYou.4: You are only looking to buy access to a founder instead of using a product

beforeAfter.title: What changes when build, recovery, and control live in one place

0.before: You are not sure whether you need to build, debug, or add controls first
0.after: You get routed to the lane that matches the real blocker

1.before: Every issue sends you into a different tool, doc, or opinion loop
1.after: Build, recovery, and control live inside one operating system

2.before: A broken setup or vague runtime behavior kills momentum
2.after: The next action is visible, reviewable, and grounded

3.before: You have an agent you cannot fully explain or trust
3.after: You have something you can run, inspect, and improve with confidence

beforeAfter.close: The goal is not to learn AI in the abstract. The goal is to get something useful working and keep it trustworthy.

pricingPage.stackBody: The offer is grounded in tracked pack docs, sequenced lessons, and live stack briefings. This route should show what is actually behind the product, not just the logo on top.
pricingPage.finalTitle: Need help choosing the honest lane?
pricingPage.finalBody: If the question is urgency, plan fit, or rollout shape, support should answer it directly and send you back into the right Pico route.

faq.title: Questions worth clearing up before you commit

0.q: Do I need to know how to code?
0.a: No. PicoMUTX is for operators who need outcomes without turning themselves into full-time agent engineers.

1.q: What if I already tried and failed?
1.a: That is one of the main entry points. PicoMUTX is built to recover momentum after the first confusing, expensive, or credibility-killing blocker.

2.q: What kind of agent work is this for?
2.a: Practical agent systems: support flows, internal copilots, lead capture, sales follow-up, and operational workflows that need to get built, fixed, or controlled in the real world.

3.q: Is there a free way to start?
3.a: Yes. Pico starts with a free trial focused on first value. You can get started immediately - no waitlist or access request needed.

4.a: PicoMUTX is the commercial wedge into MUTX, not the full enterprise governance layer. Heavier multi-team governance belongs in the broader MUTX stack.
